note: Numbers are as-of 2026-06-20 against the live OSV database. Counts drift over time; the speed ratio and result-parity finding are the durable claims. n is small (self-reported timing, warm median of a handful of runs) — this is a quick A/B, not a rigorous distribution.

metrics.label: Audit the same lockfile (warm, same OSV source)

baseline.cold_note: cold includes uvx installing pip-audit's 29 packages

metrics.ratio_desc: uv audit ~0.8s vs pip-audit ~16s warm ≈ 20x faster (both hit OSV over the network, both tool-cached)

findings.claim: Both tools find the SAME vulnerabilities.
findings.detail: Identical vuln id set (primary+alias) = 79, symmetric difference 0. Same type split: PYSEC 18 / CVE 30 / GHSA 30 / SNYK 1. Same 6 vulnerable packages: flask, idna, jinja2, pyyaml, requests, urllib3. So uv audit's value is NOT coverage — it is (1) ~20x speed, (2) native SARIF/JSON output, (3) integration with the lockfile/resolution.

findings.claim: 48 known vulnerabilities across 13 packages in the fixture.
findings.detail: `Found 48 known vulnerabilities and no adverse project statuses in 13 packages`. 6 packages vulnerable. adverse project statuses (yank/deprecated) are a separate axis, 0 here.
Vulnerabilities found -> exit 1. Clean project (deps=certifi only) -> exit 0.

findings.claim: Native SARIF 2.1.0 output for CI code-scanning.
findings.detail: `--output-format sarif` -> SARIF 2.1.0, runs[0].tool.driver.name=uv version=0.11.23, rules 48 / results 48. Added in 0.11.2x; absent from the older global 0.11.14.

findings.claim: --ignore matches by alias, suppressing linked advisory records.
findings.detail: `--ignore GHSA-562c-5r94-xh97` -> summary 48 -> 46. Also `--ignore-until-fixed` (only while no fix exists).
Emits `warning: 'uv audit' is experimental...`. The `--preview-features audit-command` flag only silences the warning; the feature works by default.

explicitly_not_verified.note: uv's malware check (a SEPARATE feature on `uv sync`, NOT on `uv audit`) was confirmed to activate and gate installs (UV_MALWARE_CHECK=1, message path verified), but a real malicious package was NOT installed to force a true-positive — deliberately, for safety. Gate behavior verified; positive detection not reproduced.
